message: Jake
Jennifer
I drive the car toward the place I heard from Gloria.
The wind lets out a growl, and the sound of waves crashing against the shore intensifies my anxiety.
Though the sky is clear, my heart is heavy, like dark clouds gather over it.
As I drive the car, I have become an unfortunate man burdened with deep sorrow.
No way.
That thought quickens my heartbeat, and gradually, the sounds fade from my ears.
In the lonely silence, what rises in my heart is
only the smiling face of her.
Every time I think of that name, my chest tightens.
Her two eyes... those were the eyes of the girl who once saved me.
The worst crime I committed in my life. She... she
lost her life protecting me.
Her sadly smiling face overlaps with that girl.
I don't remember how I got here, but I seem to have safely
arrived at her house.
Here,
could she be here?
For a moment, I worried whether I could go in, but foolishly, the door was unlocked.
Was she waiting for me?
Calling out, I entered that place,
but there was just an empty room.
The neatly organized interior, which could be seen as simple, had a spectacular view from the large window overlooking the sea.
Without any strangely gaudy elements, it was a room that felt very soothing.
The soft ivory-colored walls and a sofa draped in pastel fabrics.
A countryside home furnished in country style.
No, it reminded me of the family I lost.
It felt like a sudden illusion.
The warm home I exchanged for my grandfather's inheritance, the illusion of family being there.
I felt as if what I lost had suddenly appeared before me.
Something's not right.
I collected myself and
went back to look for it.
I stepped into the room that continued from this one.
It was a bedroom.
I called out, but there was no response.
However, from the looks of this small room,
there were no traces of them.
Suddenly, my eyes caught the nightstand by the bed.
There was something that looked like a diary placed there.
A diary?
My curiosity urged me to take that book and read it.
× month × day
Today I meet Charlie, a person introduced to me by Günter for role preparation.
He's got a great physique, but he seems terribly anxious, like a small animal. I would have preferred a woman, but I can’t ask for the impossible.
Could this be a story from my time preparing for The Structure of Madness? Still, that he was introduced to me by Günter...
× month × day
I still have dreams of tragedy. Since I was just a baby back then, there’s no way I could have clear memories.
My mother was raped by several men and ultimately beaten to death. That’s a fact that hasn't changed even now that I’ve gained fame.
Did I have such experiences in my past?
I didn’t know anything about that.
× month × day
I must accomplish what Charlie could not, in his stead.
Since I invited all of this.
Five more.
To achieve what Charlie aimed for, I need five more.
Five more, huh?
? That Charlie could not accomplish something like that?
Film work
I think it's lucky to be cast by Director Fabar. But I'm going to be tied down to New York.
It can't be helped, we'll do it in New York. I'm going to become Charlie under the skyscraper.
Does that mean he killed five women in place of Charlie?!
Finally just one more.
As long as I have the head, the Holy Mother will be resurrected.
Holy　Mother?
Are you saying you’re making the Holy Mother with parts taken from Cindy, Ginny, and Eva?!
You're insane.
This is the ramblings of a madman.
I returned the diary and headed to the room behind it.
and,
it was a small, cozy room. In the center of the room was the aforementioned coffin, and a faint scent reminiscent of lilies was in the air.
Summoning my courage, I placed my hand on the lid of the coffin,
Patricia
Even though it was expected, the scene where Patricia's face was captured filled me with a fear that seemed like it was about to rise up.
This is a man called Charlie,
and it is the Holy Mother completed by his hands.
But why is it that,
I wonder if I ended up doing something like this?
I
I searched the whole house for her.
Where is she?
Why did she have to do something like that?
That's what I wanted to hear.
Finding the staircase to the second floor, I felt like she was up there, so I rushed up the stairs.
It was a bright room with a wall completely made of windows.
And sure enough, she was there—not in the room, but in a cliff-like place outside.
I found my way outside and walked towards her.
